title: Simple Step Stool with Straight Legs
summary: A single-step stool with flat straight legs and a triangular jigsaw cutout — a much smaller, faster build than this catalog's housed-dado Shaker step stool.
description:
Where this catalog's Shaker step stool relies on housed dados for a fastener-free joint, this is the fast, casual version: flat 1x10 legs with a triangular hand-hold cutout, joined to a 1x3 support with straightforward screws. It's a good weekend-afternoon build for a stool that will see light, occasional use rather than daily standing.

Cut the legs to length, then cut the triangular hand-hold shape out of each leg with a jigsaw — this cutout is decorative and functional at once, giving the stool a lift-and-carry point. Attach the support pieces to the legs with either straightforward screws or, if you have a jig, pocket holes drilled to face upward on the inside faces so they'll accept the top in the next step invisibly.

Attach the top last, either with straightforward screws through the top into the legs, or by driving up through the predrilled pocket holes if you went that route. **This is a genuinely simple stool** — appropriate for reaching a top shelf or helping a child wash hands at a sink, not intended as a ladder substitute for serious height or heavy-duty standing.

materials.name: Self-tapping wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

cutList.note: Triangular hand-hold cutout in each, cut with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Cut the parts
steps.body: Cut the legs, supports, and top according to the cut list, using a miter saw or jigsaw. Cut the triangular hand-hold shape out of each leg with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Attach the supports to the legs
steps.body: Attach the support pieces to the legs with 2" screws. If using pocket holes, drill 3/4" pocket holes facing upward on the insides of the legs and supports for attaching the top, plus pocket holes on the ends of the supports, and attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Attach the top and finish
steps.body: Attach the top to the base with 2" screws, or through the predrilled pocket holes with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws if using that method. Sand smooth, rounding edges, and finish.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

unresolvedImages.alt: Small step stool with flat straight legs and a triangular hand-hold cutout
